headline: Government Contracts: 98.4% Awarded to Bumiputera Companies in 2024
context: Treasury data shows 98.4% of direct-negotiation government contracts in 2024 were awarded to Bumiputera-owned companies, totalling RM62B out of RM63B.

cards.big: 98.4% of direct-negotiation government contracts went to Bumiputera companies.
cards.sub: RM62B out of RM63B. Open tender represented only 28% of total procurement.
The Procurement Guideline allows direct award for 'national interest' — a category with no defined limits.

cards.big: Auditor General found 34% of direct-negotiation contracts had cost overruns exceeding 30%.
Article 153 mentions permits and licences, not procurement quotas.
cards.sub: The procurement policy is administrative, not constitutional. It could be reformed without amending the constitution.

cards.big: 98.4% is not a quota. It is a monopoly with a different label.
Procurement reform does not require abandoning Bumiputera participation. It requires open tendering with Bumiputera incentives — preference, not exclusion. Competition and inclusion can coexist.
